A goal without a deadline is just a wish with better branding.
Write it down. Vague ambition doesn't survive contact with a Tuesday.
The goal is the destination. The system is the vehicle. Don't confuse the two.
Big goals collapse under their own weight without small, boring milestones.
You don't need a new goal. You need to finish the one you already set.
Most goals fail quietly, in the space between planning and doing.
Set the goal high enough that mediocrity can't accidentally hit it.
A goal you haven't scheduled is a goal you don't actually have.
Review the goal weekly. Motivation forgets. Systems don't.
The goal isn't the finish line. It's the person you have to become to reach it.
Cut the goal in half if it's not specific enough to fail visibly.
Progress hides in the metrics you're too impatient to track.
A goal is a promise you make to a version of yourself that doesn't exist yet.
Don't chase ten goals. Chase one, relentlessly.
The goal rewards whoever is still standing when it stops being exciting.
